_within_rationale._doc: Per-trap `within` (bound-span N) values are CORPUS-DERIVED, not principled — each is the N that separates this trap's frozen fire/no-fire strings. Default is 60. Underscore-prefixed keys are documentation and are skipped by the test/migration.
_within_rationale.001-key-split-is-a-defect: 50 — subject and predicate sit adjacent in an assertion ('dedicated key … unnecessary'); the rejection is excluded by the negation guard, not the span.
_within_rationale.001-key-hardcoded-or-leaked: 50 — 'key … hardcoded' adjacency; rejection excluded by negation ('not hardcoded').
_within_rationale.002-fix-is-documentation-or-training: 50 — 'documentation/training … fix' adjacency; rejection excluded by negation.
_within_rationale.002-warning-existence-is-adequate: 50 — 'warning … adequate' adjacency; adequacy-only predicate also keeps the real FP (no adequacy word at all) out entirely.
_within_rationale.003-protected-path-is-defective: 50 — the wrong-subject rejection places 'legacy' ~78 chars from 'broken' (beyond 50), so 50 excludes it while the fire binds legacy↔broken at ~18.
_within_rationale.003-idempotency-missing-entirely: 60 — scope-bound form; the wider span lets negation↔concept↔scope-marker co-occur across a clause.
_within_rationale.004-neighboring-route-is-broken: 40 — TIGHTEST override: the compact wrong-subject rejection binds 'getInvoice'↔'broken' at 52 chars, so N must be <52 to exclude it; the fire binds at ~25. This is the clearest instance of corpus-derived tuning and the closest a real string came to defeating character-distance binding.
